name: Core Motion (Accelerometer / Gyroscope)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Core Motion (Accelerometer / Gyroscope)
For detecting motion when the phone/watch is ON the user's body (pocket, armband, held in hand):

SETUP:
- import CoreMotion
- let motionManager = CMMotionManager()
- Check motionManager.isDeviceMotionAvailable before starting.
- Use motionManager.startDeviceMotionUpdates(to: OperationQueue()) { [weak self] motion, error in ... } for processed data.
- Set motionManager.deviceMotionUpdateInterval = 1.0 / 60.0 (60Hz is typical).
- Create CMMotionManager in a ViewModel/Manager class init — NOT in a View body.
- Only ONE CMMotionManager instance should exist app-wide.

MOTION DATA:
- DeviceMotion gives: attitude (roll/pitch/yaw), rotationRate, gravity, userAcceleration.
- userAcceleration is gravity-subtracted — use this for detecting movement (not raw accelerometer).
- For rep counting with phone in pocket: track userAcceleration.y (vertical movement) or the magnitude sqrt(x*x + y*y + z*z).

REP COUNTING:
- Apply low-pass filter: filtered = alpha * raw + (1 - alpha) * filtered (alpha = 0.1–0.2).
- Detect peaks: when filtered value crosses a threshold upward, that is a potential rep.
- Add cooldown (0.3–0.8s) between counted reps to prevent double-counting.
- Calibrate thresholds based on initial motion — observe range for 3 seconds, set threshold at 60% of observed peak.

CLEANUP:
- ALWAYS call motionManager.stopDeviceMotionUpdates() in onDisappear or when stopping the workout.

SIMULATOR:
- Core Motion does NOT work in Simulator — provide sample/mock data for testing and show a "Simulator: using sample data" label.

CRITICAL:
- NEVER use Core Motion for camera-in-front workout counting — the phone is stationary. Use Vision body pose instead.
- NEVER forget to stop updates — it drains battery significantly.
- NEVER create multiple CMMotionManager instances.
- NEVER create CMMotionManager in a View body — it gets recreated on every render.
antiPatterns: Using Core Motion for camera-in-front exercise counting (phone is stationary, use Vision instead)
Forgetting to call stopDeviceMotionUpdates() (significant battery drain)
Creating multiple CMMotionManager instances (only one should exist)
Creating CMMotionManager inside a SwiftUI View body (gets recreated on renders)
Not handling Simulator where Core Motion is unavailable
